name: Kadin Gobegi (Ladies' Navels)
recipeIngredient: Whipped cream or kaymak (kaymak is clotted cream)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve sugar in water over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Bring to a boil, add lemon juice, and boil rapidly for 15 minutes without stirring. Remove syrup from the pan and let it cool.
2. Sift flour and salt onto a square of stiff paper.
3. Heat water and butter in another heavy pan until boiling. Pour in flour all at once, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon or balloon whisk. Keep stirring until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan, then cook on low heat for an additional 5 minutes. Remove roux from heat and cool for 2 minutes.
4. Gradually beat eggs into the mixture. Add almond essence and beat until smooth and satiny.
5. Oil hands and roll dough into balls about the size of a walnut. Shape each ball into a round about 5 cm (2 inches) in diameter by pressing an oiled forefinger into the center to make a hole. Keep hands oiled during shaping to prevent sticking.
6. Heat oil in a large electric frypan to a depth of 1 cm (1/2 inch). Place half of the shaped gobegi into the hot oil. Increase heat to 200°C (400°F) as soon as they are added.
7. When the gobegi rise to the surface and puff, turn them over. Fry for 15 minutes total, turning frequently during the last half of cooking time to ensure even browning.
8. Remove cooked gobegi from oil with a slotted spoon and drain briefly on paper towels. Place in syrup and let sit for 5 minutes before transferring to a plate.
9. Turn off frypan and allow oil to cool before adding the second batch.
10. To serve, arrange gobegi on a flat platter and place a dollop of whipped cream or kaymak in the center. Sprinkle with pistachio nuts.
Variation: For Variation, follow the above steps but flatten the dough balls to about 6 cm (2-1/2 inches) round and fold over so that it resembles lips on a curved edge. Fry and finish as for Kadin Gobegi. Serve plain or with whipped cream or kaymak.
